reviews.review_text:
My dearest message to Destiny 2. You gave me lots of great memories and moments with friends and randoms, such beautiful atmospheres, dynamic music, amazing gunplay, deep lore, and worldbuilding. The music and atmosphere are definitely still at the top of my list to this day. The grind was too much, but you decided how to play the game and experimented with many build combos, and each build felt special as long as it worked out for you. My best and favorite time during D2 was the 30th Anniversary through post-Witch Queen and Into the Light.

The game's direction started going downhill after the introduction of Destiny Content Vault. Newer players wouldn't even know what is happening in the story because of this and would have to rely on YouTube or someone they know who played this game since its release. The New Light campaign is a HORRIBLE experience; seeing my friends who joined Destiny after the DCV was so confusing for them to understand the story.

The Final Shape should have been the final conclusion to this game or continue the next chapter in D3. Now we ended off on a Star Wars expansion... which honestly wasn't TOO bad, I suppose, compared to Edge of Fate, but it felt like a mindless storyline going nowhere. There are still many things left to do lore-wise from past seasons/content, the main reason I still held on to this game after TFS.

Destiny as an entire franchise is a solid 9/10.

Per Audacia Ad Astra <3

Destiny 2 was never just a game to me.

It was a place where time disappeared without warning. A universe filled with late nights, familiar voices, victories that felt bigger than they should have, and silence that sometimes said more than words ever could.

Somewhere along the way, this game taught me things beyond mechanics and raids. It showed me patience, connection, disappointment, and the strange way virtual worlds can leave very real marks on a person.

There are memories here that I will always carry with me, not because they were perfect, but because they were real.

And maybe that is why saying goodbye feels heavier than expected. Because in the end, we are not only leaving behind a game; we are leaving behind versions of ourselves that once existed inside it.

Still, I do not look back with bitterness.

Only gratitude.

For every moment that made this world feel alive.
For every lesson that stayed with me after the screen went dark.
And for the reminder that even temporary places can become permanent memories.

Thank you, Destiny 2.

For everything.

If this really is your end, thanks for the great memories little light. 

Still a good game to play here and there if you have friends and want to experience a top-tier gun play design.

Rest in peace Destiny 2. Best damn game I've ever played, so many priceless memories playing this game.
Per Audacia Ad Astra.
This game changed my life and i will be standing on business if we don't get d3 greenlit i am not touching anything Sony. if your with me smash that like button
Destiny died so Marathon could die. After Marathon, Bungie's next title will be an RPG game called Layoffs. Inspired by true events.

As someone who has over 2000 hours on Destiny 1 and almost 9000 hours on D2 between Console and PC I would not recommend this game as of right now. They destroyed the replayability and core of the game and took all the heart out of Destiny to fund Marathon instead of investing in a Destiny 3. Truly sad to see my favourite game/franchise of all time to fall so far.

Dont be sad its ending
Be happy that it happened

This game is ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥, buts its MY ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ </3

its hard to express in words how i feel about d2 after all this time so ill just share an experience. when i first started the game i saw the video of prof broman starting d2 day 1 bungie showed him his achievments and clears with his teammates and date and he was crying happy tears i didnt get it then i thought thats a bit much but when that eventual d3 hits and they show me my past achievements video i think ill be bawling my eyes out. some of the best memories in gaming have been from this game some i dont think ill ever forget, it gave me so many lifelong friends but all things must come to an end i hope to come back to my charecters one day i hope my friends come back too but if this is truly the end thats alright aswell d2 has done its job and then some it brought people together like no other game
Destiny raised me when my parents didn't want to. Say what you want about the game, but at its core there was never anything like it
There will never be another game quite like destiny. I will continue to play raids until it is no longer possible. I have the highest hopes for June 9th. Love you all.

Started at Witch Queen, and what I can say, is this was one of my favorite games of all time, only for Bungie to ruin it. Thank you to the community and Devs for this awesome game. For the upper management, you suck, you got greedy, and ruined a fantastic game.

After 12 long years, Bungie has announced [url=https://old.reddit.com/r/DestinyTheGame/comments/1tjqpzd/destiny_2_every_end_is_a_new_beginning/]an end to Destiny's live service.[/url]

Yet, many would argue that this game has long since been on life support; gasping & writhing, season after overpriced season until it has finally been given a compassionate, albeit belated, release.

Now that development has finally ceased and the "final shape" has coalesced, I can look back at the exact moment when I decided to quit. All the way back in the year 2020, when Bungie announced the Destiny Content Vault (DCV) and I can unequivocally say,

"I made the right choice."

I don't intend to argue that my thousands of hours of time invested was not well wasted. While there has certainly been contentious controversies content-wise, the core experience has always been fundamentally enthralling. Destiny may come seasoned with dollops of anti-consumer slop but it still holds a Pavlovian death grip on the addicted appetites of enjoyers & haters alike.

Thankfully, there is no better cure for overcoming withdrawal than coming to terms with the fact that "live service" is ephemeral. DCV & sunsetting has tirelessly echoed time & time again across Destiny discourse in the six years since its inception. There is no question that its consequences have left an indelible psychic scar on community opinions.

The final triumphant hurrah of Destiny comes with a myriad of familiar features (i.e. Return of the Director, Pantheon 2.0, etc.) but the true knell is in what has been left unsaid. The Red War, eternally consigned to the dust of memory; pinnacle raids, languishing and never to be reprised again; the new player experience buried beneath an incoherent debt of half-remembered systems and an indifferent dismissal to go watch a YouTube recap instead.

The thing with endings is that it's an end. No more opportunities for salvation, no do-overs, no retries. All the copium that was compulsively inhaled over the years has amounted to one final rasping gasp. This is Destiny as it will ever be because, honestly, after all of this are you going to trust Bungie's attempt at Destiny 3? If you do, I'm sure they'll be more than happy for you to repurchase the D2 raids.

Alcoholics Anonymous is a lifetime membership because sobriety is a continuous commitment. I thumb my six year chip and think to myself,

"Thank you, Bungie. It's over because there's nothing worth going back to."

[spoiler]If you're interested in seeing how much time you've [strike]spent[/strike] wasted in Destiny 2, check out this [url=https://d2scrub.land/]site[/url] by Chris Fried.[/spoiler]

Oh boy.

As of June, Destiny 2 will end. The servers won't be offline and you can still play it, but it is going to be in maintenance mode officially. Because of this, it is not recommended to get into this game now or buy any of the Expansion content. With a rapidly dwindling playerbase, you also will have more difficulty getting into raids or dungeons unless you have friends to play with.

Everything about playing the game feels amazing, and maybe it's worth downloading the free version to give it a try. I just don't think it's worth trying to get invested in something that doesn't have a future anymore and has a lot of unanswered questions left in the story.

D2 was never just a game, it felt like home. A place I could always return to, not just for the gameplay, but for the community. No other game ever captured me the way Destiny did. The world, music, lore and the feeling of stepping into each new expansion not knowing what waited ahead. It always felt special.

I’m heartbroken to see this chapter end, but more than anything I’m grateful for every adventure, every memory, and every friend I met along the way.

Thank you for the journey. See you starside :(<3

After over 4000 hours across both Destiny 1 and 2, the series finally comes to an end. The fun, memories and highs far outweigh the drama, the mistakes and the lows. This game was a significant part of my life, with many friends made along the way.

As it stands now, since there will be no more updates in the future, i do recommend this game. Just make sure to get a friend that knows what they're doing to play with you. Otherwise, you'll need to read tons of content online, but the passionate community has also left plenty of information on all quests, levels and activities during these last 12 years that this might not be that big of a problem. Other than that, it's a blast, tons of weapons, abilities, builds and things to do. The soundtrack also still kicks major ass. 

Finally, i highly recommend raiding at least once. My best experiences in this game have been in this activity. 

Shame it will end this way, but i guess it's for the best.

See you starside.
Guardians make their own fate.

"Knew this day would come, and with it, one last lesson…
There's an end to all things, kid. Good and bad.
Sure, the best times seem small, and the bad tend to linger, but the only permanent is eternity.
-
It's yours now, 'til the last flame dies and all words've been spoken.
'Til that time.
Safe journeys. Straight aim. And good huntin'."

The Last Word

Thanks for one hell of an amazing journey, there will never be a game like Destiny 2, I'm grateful for all the adventures I got to share with friends new and old. Eyes up guardian.

There will never be another game like Destiny, it's tragic to see it come to an end but all good things eventually do. 


Eyes up, Guardian.

Years of mismanagement has finally laid Destiny 2 to rest. Greed, corruption and a decade of poor decision making have caught up. I won't say Marathon was the one to kill D2, but it appears to be a factor. I had personally given up on this game years ago, but no time like the present to officially leave my thumbs down. 

It was a good time before thr sunset, and now it seems the darkness has taken over. RIP.

i've done it all in this game: pvp tournaments, pve speedruns, challenges, solo raids, paid live carries, you can pretty much name it and i've done it in the past decade. people will most often comment on gunplay and community but two facts will always remain true: the community is infested with the most wretched disgusting people who will always outnumber and smother the positive light. the gunplay has and will ever only feel good due to unfathomable amounts of aim assistance on both input methods and obscenely generous client sided netcode, favoring the physics host of the activity. this is (generously) assuming the 5% of the gameplay a weapon is actually being fired

despite these facts, I still enjoyed the nearly ten year experience. the game was unfortunately assassinated from the inside by corporate greed, (seemingly) poor leadership, weak design direction and the fact that top management at bungie was mostly ran by those that care more about yearly bonuses and vintage cars rather than making a passionate product. this game and franchise quite honestly, does not deserve to be concluded yet

play it, with friends only, and only if you are getting the full game including all expansions for a ludicrous sale price below a AAA title price

Attention all Guardians! 
I think you all heard the news. It is truly sad to see such a great game go down in such an unnecessary manner. 
Sure, Destiny 2 had its ups and downs (sadly over the last couple of years a somewhat persistent down trend) but because of the incredible irreplaceable memories that were forged playing this game, and the good friends I made along the way, I can’t just stand by and watch when one of my favorite Games is dying. I am pretty sure lots of you fellow Guardians that can relate. 
Maybe it is naive but I want to believe that this is not over, that this is not the end. To avert the current destined outcome, I think I know what we have to do. Gather as many Guardians as possible and join me on June 9th when the last update drops, we rally and show them that we care.
If our experience as Guardians has taught us one thing, it is that Guardians make their own fate. We will not just give up! 
See you starside on June 9th Guardian. Per Audacia Ad Astra.

It may be officially over now, but this has been a really fun nine years of playing this game. It has had many of its downs, as I am not delusional enough to think otherwise, but I had a really good time with what it had to offer. I speak more privileged than others since I was able to play all of its content from the beginning and throughout its history, but genuinely, it was one of the most fun games I've played. I enjoyed the grind, the very enigmatic story (lol), met a bunch of nice people. As of this review, I will still be playing the game, it's just that obviously there's less reason to do so after its big June update has been thoroughly gone through. I find it undeniable that they put their eggs in the wrong basket with making Marathon. I don't hate it, but it's written all over the stars that it ended the current iteration of Destiny and I can't believe Sony has preferred babying Marathon over the Destiny franchise. It had a lot of potential, so many things that never got explored, and never will. Destiny players (especially ex-Destiny players) love to be very vocal and negative when it comes to this game, but I won't. I appreciated and enjoyed everything I got to play. It was a big part of my casual life and I don't regret a single minute playing it. I only wish that Steam had an "in the middle" option on recommending a game for obvious reasons.

From a veteran player from the beginning,

See you all starside.

I've played D2 on and off for it's entire life cycle, not because I don't enjoy the game but because the studio genuinely made playing the game awful at certain points of it's lifetime. Their mantra of bare subsistence past major content drops and the removal or limiting of content has always been the flashpoint of contention between the player base and developers but at the end of the day they've crafted one of the most unique and rewarding gameplay loops in the history of video games, I don't think anyone can dispute that. The raids, dungeons, special missions and lore prove that what we have here is something truly special. I even have a top 3 clear in the world on a dungeon because I played this game that much and that hard.

As of 5/21/2026 Bungie announced that they're giving a final EoS update to the game so they can move onto supporting Marathon and new IP, and I for one and am moving on from Bungie. This marks the 2nd time they've moved on from a major FPS franchise they've created and left their player base hung dry. Why should I, as the consumer and the one who you have to retain at the end of the pipeline, stick around so you pull the next fleece in 10 years time? You did it with Halo, you're doing it with Destiny right now, your record PROVES you will do it with Marathon. My time, effort, and money have clearly not been respected by any of you.

I never like saying people should lose their job, because their are so many talented devs that have graced Bungie you can even see this in a sub-par product like Concord having ex-staff worked on that game, but people who managed and "lead" at this studio need to be fired and blacklisted if there is any hope of salvaging the future. Their corporate rot and mismanagement have turned one of the biggest MMORPG's of the past decade into a barren husk of what was just to pump and dump a Japanese corporation for a few billion dollars before the ride crashed and burned completely. Pete Parsons needs to be audited and investigated for ineptitude and malicious compliance.

Final score 6/7 needed more funny Telesto bugs and less greedy upper management.

I don't normally write reviews for games since it is not something I do. If I enjoy the game I enjoy it, if I don't then I refund and leave it. 

However, Destiny was a franchise where I felt both joy and hatred for so many years. I remember secretly playing this game on a school night getting through the first mission. My first FPS game and the first time I have found a community playing a game. 

Years later, this game still holds a special place in my heart where I always recommend friends to get back into playing or play for the first time. Seeing the recent news broke me as this was the game I grew up with my entire life. Hopefully we get more news of its future but for now...

Thank you Destiny

It genuinely pains me to lower my rating for Destiny 2. This is a game that has consumed thousands of hours of my life, and I’ve loved almost every second of it… but the experience for new players is heartbreaking.
If Bungie were to assemble a team to streamline the story for new Lights, this could be a game that truly pulls in more players. Right now, though? There’s almost no direction for a fresh face. New players arrive at the Tower, and suddenly they’re drowning in 30 quests with barely a hint of guidance. I’ve even played through the game on a second account to see what a “new Light” experiences, and it’s painful: so much of the story is reduced to short videos, and the legacy quests clutter the quest log with no sense of order. Players are thrust directly into the latest expansion without context, without the journey that made me fall in love with this world in the first place.
A while back, when expansions were nearly 90% off, a friend asked if they should buy the game. I could not, in good faith, tell them yes. I wanted to—the heart wants to share this universe—but I couldn’t. This is a game that once gave me some of the most rewarding moments of my gaming life, yet I fear that for new players, it might just feel like a confusing, fragmented mess. And that reality… it truly breaks my heart.

The most amazing game ever created in the entire world of worlds. Bungie hates this game, they killed it for no reason, it had the most potential of any game ever, bungie could've become the first trillion dollar studio if they put 1% more effort into actually exploring everything in this game. There is no expanded clan system, for no reason at all. There is no care for actual achievements in the community, solo flawless raids mean nothing except for a little tick on raid report. They created amazing activities only to either forever abandon them or completely remove them after a seasonal cycle. They continually let balancing in pvp and pve harm each other and make the game worse in every way. They vaulted some of the best content in the game, and the best content for onboarding new players. THEY CONTANSTLY WERE CONFUSED ON WHY THEY COULDN'T EASILY ONBOARD NEW PLAYERSA< THEY RELEASSED THE WORSTR THING EVER EVER EVER FEVE MADE CALLED THE PORTAL TO ONBOARD NERW PLAYERS AND IT WAS SO ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ AWFUL THAY IT COMPLETELY KILLED THE GAME. THEY ABANDONED THE BIGGEST CASH COW OF A GAME FOR MARATHO. WHO EVER EVE RTHOGUHT OR PRESENETED THE IDEA OF MARATHON, DESTROY THEM, END THEM. I can't wait for destiny 3 when i'm 500 years old
RIP to one of the best games ever made, horribly fumbled but not forgotten. This game has had some of the highest highs and the lowest lows but its always made for great memories.

I started playing this game during Season of the Plunder, which to this day is considered one of the lowest points of the game. When I started playing, I was met with overwhelmingly negative sentiment towards the game by the friends I had made through the game. Thankfully though, I didn't care; I was so in love with the mechanics of the gameplay and how well Bungie managed to execute the power fantasy aspect of MMOs in the form of a shooter. Still to this day, this is something that no other developer has even come close to in my opinion. 

I have sunk an abhorrent amount of time into this game, far more than any other game, which if you know me, you know that is difficult. I think there is still potential for Destiny beyond the second game if Bungie and Sony choose to continue moving in that direction. There really is nothing like playing raids in Destiny. While some other MMOs have raids on par with Destiny's, none of them are FPSs and not only does Destiny manage to incorporate the FPS element into the MMO space with such fluidity that you would think this was a popular genre of game, it takes it and uses it to make the game its own. The raid mechanics, while monotonous at times due to the limitations imposed by it being a shooter, provide for some of the best group experiences you can have on any game.  

After spending some months playing the game, I became aware of the idea of Raid Racing and was instantly consumed by the idea. I met some great people, and some not so great in the pursuit of trying to get a higher number on a website the community hailed as holy scripture. This community is ultimately what led to me really falling in love with the game. Throughout my time playing the game, I met people that were and still are way better than I am, grown with people who were also pursuing this meaningless number and developed a better understanding of the game development industry. 

Unfortunately, I started the game after what most people considered to be the prime of the game, but I still had a ton of fun with the game. At its core, Destiny 2 is a great game that was drove into the ground by suits that were grossly out-of-touch with the average player, looking to squeeze any drop of profit out of the game. There was drip-feeding of content, false promises and often times, complete neglect of the community.  Majority of this is not the developers fault and unlike a lot of people at the moment, I feel for all of the Bungie employees that are going to inevitably be laid off following the announcement. "Wokeness" did not kill Destiny, greedy executives did. With that being said, I will forever be grateful for the amazing people I met through this game. 

#FVKPETEPARSONS

Well
This is it.

Nearly 12 years since this franchise first opened up to us.
12 years since I woke up in the Cosmodrome.
12 years since I started a game that would save me.

It's a bittersweet feeling. An ending a long time coming, writing that's been on the wall for months now, years even, if you listen to some. Memories made, gods slain, friendships forged, triumphs conquered.

I don't regret the time and money I poured into this franchise, even if it was too much. I can only wish the best for the devs in the future, and a hope that someday this grand universe will return.
It's a faint hope, but its mine.

It's been a good run. See you starside, guardians.

Grātiās vōbīs agimus.

No other game will replace the amount of connection this game had made in my life. Aside from the upper echelon shenanigans, It was still a great game, Good story beats (for the most part) and a fun sandbox to just go out test your abilities and skill. Destiny will always be with us in the end. Sad to see it go but, I don't regret a single moment of playing this game with my fire team.

I have played this game for twelve years almost half of my life, while there have been other games to pull away my attention and hold on to me for a while. Nothing ever quite feels the same as coming back to Destiny, throughout all the ups and downs this game has helped me in ways I never thought would happen, I made friends, I kept friends, I showed the way for so many new players, and fell in love with a story about space wizards with guns fighting aliens. This game saved my life many times over with how amazing the community is and how supportive they are regardless of their walks of life. I will forever cherish memories I have of this game... From the first time I awoke in the cosmodrome, to the first time I cleared the salvations edge surrounded by friends and new friends. This game did something special in bringing people together. It is by no means perfect but I think that's what everyone needed was hope that even though now it isn't amazing we all knew it could be. Like something to hope for every day you logged in. While I have been distant from this game for the last year and half seeing it die like this is devasting and bitter sweet. All good things must come to an end but having to say goodbye to something that meant so much to some many people: a distraction from the world, a community to always talk to, a story that pulled on hearts and was a bit goofy and dysfunctional at times, gun play that somehow always felt so satisfying, a game to make number go big, a game where you could play slow or fast, but always an expectation that we were all in this together a sense of pride in what class you played, a sense of fashion in every guardian, and lastly a hope for the next day to be as good as the last or better. Thank you for all the years and memories and challenges and fun...See you all star side
overall gunplay is the best I've ever felt but literally everything else is god awful except raids

I love this game and I will miss it. It was an honor to play this game for as long as I was able, even when it was in a bad state, which felt pretty frequent lol. This game will always be a part of me and I am grateful for the experiences I was able to have with friends and stories I was able to enjoy. Thank you to everyone for playing this mess of a game and thank you to the devs for supporting as long as you could.

Keep making your own fate Guardians. <3

D2 has been a run for me, It's not my main game anymore, but it was a big thing for me for quite a while. Now having seen that the last content update coming up, it's a bittersweet farewell. I don't know how much I would recommend to a new player, most of my favorite times in Destiny, you had to be there for at the time and I think most of the stuff coming in June, will be fun for someone new but won't really have the same meaning unless you were a long time player.

This game has always been here for me whenever I needed it. Ive played every release day one and through all its up and downs im glad to have been able to experience it when the future looked bright. It upsets me to know that I will never get that new release excitement anymore and that the IP ended in the way that it did. Im hoping that maybe, one day I will get to experience that new destiny feeling again whenever that might be.   "Eyes up Guardian"

The only world in gaming I can say, for better or worse, that I truly loved for all the time I was able to spend in it. An unmatched feeling that I may unfortunately be left hoping for until the day I die.

Never was the absolute best, nor was it ever the worst, but it meant so much the whole way. Such a shame to see it go out in such an ill-fitting way.

 May the loss of this game usher in an indescribable sense of guilt and regret to those who brought it low and may the weight of future expectations crush you.

Destiny has gone through the highest highs and the lowest lows of any game I've played,  its always been there for me when i needed it the most, there were times I hated playing it but the fond memories I've made by playing this game outweigh all the bad ones. Thank you Bungie for making this incredible rollercoaster of emotions we call Destiny, my favorite game of all time - " You're my favorite. Don't ever forget that. "-   Cayde-6
Per Audacia Ad Astra.

We will all look back on this game with rose tinted glasses. Remembering the good times. Never forget it was people that don't play games that ran this into the ground. Never forget it was people that don't play games that purchased this company or sold it, over, and over, and over again. Never forget passion dies at the stock market, replaced with a $ sign and a corporate suit every quarter. Find games that are run by gamers, not suits.

This game was poisoned by suits, and put down by them too. While the devs with great ideas or revolutionary decisions were disregarded for "the safe profit". Cheers to the years Dev-Team sorry for layoffs while your game was taken out back by Sony and put down, or parked in a garage with Pete's car collection built on layoffs.
This game died so that Marathon could die along with it. Moronic devs, moronic leadership that doesn't care about their players

after all the time and hours put into what started as a genuinely fun game, they outright shut it down and just turn to focus on other things because they failed to realize that vaulting content that people pay for was actually super harmful to the community. such a great story wasted on devs who know nothing of their own fanbase.

Destiny 2 gave me some of the best enjoyment I've had with a game.. and yet.

I watched, I played, I had fun during the games (in my mind) Golden Age. Then I watched the friends and other players I played a lot with leave the game, and yet I stuck with it, met other players I had fun with, watched those leave and found another group just to watch those leave as well. 

Then the Sunsetting Age began and I started questioning myself why I was still playing. With everyone I knew gone, and the daunting, nerve wrecking and anxiously filled task of looking for a new clan every season.. every update.. every new content.. I took a break myself. Yet somehow a while later I found myself back in the game.
This time I thought I would just go solo, see where it would take me. That was the worst struggle I've gone through with this game.. Everything felt like it was designed to only be fun and playable with friends, and having the memories of previous experience of finding groups constantly for every content and a raging anxiety, that made it impossible to keep going.

Then came the Abusive Age.
Overpriced expansions and DLC, shallow content deliveries that couldnt match the price, weekly story you could only experience in the week it was live and then gone forever.. Content coming and going. Worlds and stories blinked from existence. It was impossible to keep up unless I'd exclusively dedicate it all to a game that no longer felt inviting.
I tried. I honestly tried to enjoy this game again so many times, clinging to the golden memories of it I once had, wanting to experince those again. But that's all that was left, the longing for a game that no longer existed. 
Everything I loved about this game was gutted by Bongo a long time ago.

Learning of the games fate, going into its life support age, knowing it was eventually inevitable, recommending a game that lost what made it so special to me a long time ago is just not possible.

I'm also just learning its unplayable on linux, so any thoughts of ever going back for nostalgia in the future are gone..

You know.. for all the dislike and faults it had, which I understand, I lost count how many times I deleted and remade a character just to replay the Red War campaign. This game had me hooked back then.


tl;dr
The game I knew is gone, BONGO lost what made them good and got greedy.. way too greedy.
Still too pricy (and very much an understatement) in its current state and content that was paid for yeeted out of existence.
What is left at the time of this writing, I wouldnt touch or go back to again. Not that I could anyway with no way to play on linux. I've loved this game to a fault, and thoroughly and quietly hated BONGO for what they've made it into.

Per Audacia Ad Astra, may we see each other starside again some day.. Hopefully.

D1 veteran, with a few more hours on the clock than Steam says.

I always thought about writing a review, but never did. But here I am.

Of course, Destiny had its lows and its highs. Some things were absolutely awful, like the new player experience, especially because they removed the campaign that properly introduced players to the game. But the raids were on a whole different level. No game has ever delivered raids in an FPS like this.

There are plenty of videos on YouTube for those who want to know the details, even if every opinion may differ from another. But I have to say:

This game was my third space. A place I called home. A place where I found peace, and which made me happy on rough days. A place where I found real-life friends and where we had so much fun together. The game itself feels vibrant and rich, the lore is insanely deep, and the gameplay is very satisfying once you get even the basics rolling.

Would I say it was a good game for people to hop into? Before a certain point, yes, absolutely. But it got tough after Shadowkeep, if I remember correctly.

All in all, I absolutely love this game, and I’m very sad about the recent news. Still would recommend, especially if you want something new to deep dive into. I will be playing on June 9th, when the final update is arrving. And if someone needs a little help, feel free to ping me.

Per audacia ad astra!

I remember getting this game on Christmas so excited to play it, I’ve grown up with this game, it’s been with me for my teenage years and even into adulthood, it was quite sad to watch the decline of possibly my most favourite and loved game of all time, no one will ever capture the magic this game had, it is something that just can’t be replicated, I’ll forever cherish the memories I’ve made on this game, I’ll still play this game until they turn the servers off but all good things must come to an end eventually, I just hope this isn’t the end of the destiny series as a whole, I’ll never forget you destiny, see you starside cowboy. 

“You’re my favourite, don’t ever forget that.”

Back in 2018-2019 this game was one of the best MMOs you could play, especially if you liked shooters. A staggering amount of content in the form of campaigns, raids, strikes, world events, competitive pvp scene and one of the coolest new player on-boarding's ever (remember the elemental tree quests? I do). 

This is no longer the case as a large amount of that content is gone and gone for good. 

D2 is done on June 9th and D3 is not coming. Marathon is a flop and I don't care what they add, they aren't going to save it. 

Very Ironic that Marathon was the IP that lead bungie down the road to Halo, now Marathon is IP that will lead them down the road to liquidation. It was a blast guardians, even back on Battle.net

After the news this game is going into maintenance mode, I stand by that I love this game to death, I wish it succeeded, I wish it had scooped the potential that it had. All for Bungie's poor decisions to get the better of them and this is the result. If they just had their head out of their rear, then maybe, just maybe this game would've survived and giving the things people wanted years ago would've kept this game going and Marathon would've been good. But sadly this is what comes to pass.

I love this game, same with Destiny 1, I grew up with these games, and now this is probably one of if not the last of my childhood games to be thrown away like trash. The changes they put in are some things we NEEDED YEARS AGO, but NOW we get them? As they shut the doors to new expansions and updates, we finally get what we've been asking for after a very long time only for the monkeys paw to come full circle.

I'm sorry, I really love this game, but you can't play more than 50% of the content and the story will make 0 sense to anyone seeing this and wanting to try. It's just not worth the trouble for new players anymore. For some who want to come back from time to time and play one last crucible match, one last raid or dungeon run before not looking at this game, that's what I'll do, then my doors to this game will forever be shut, after the last two disgraceful DLC's we got, I'm never buying those, but I'll forever still come back to the end of the light and dark saga and see that this was how it should've ended and this other new stuff shouldn't even be here aside from the good changes made to it. That is all, thank you for all of the fond and very great memories that were given, but no longer will I ever give my trust to this studio again.

I took a break. Praying things would get better and of course, it didn't. Again I'll play one last time, maybe come back every so often, after almost a year of not playing, it felt great, I'll still play for this last send off, then I'll touch Destiny 2 again when I feel like it, but I don't think that'll take long at all for it's doors to be shut and we can't be open to play soon anyway.

I never make reviews because I don't ever feel the need to. I for once feel the need to.

This game and series has had it's up and downs galore. But there has never been a game with so much potential squandered away.

At it's core this is an amazing game, but that simply isn't enough. The Developers have consistently disregarded their community to the downfall of the game. 

I will encourage any reading this to get the game and pray there's enough people online to fully enjoy it and all the endgame content. It's all very well made and fun with the right people and mindset.

I simply cannot leave a positive rating due to the lack of attention and respect from the devs. They killed this game for a worse one, didn't listen and heed our words at all over the years. 

Per Audacia Ad Astra Guardians both old and new.

The onboarding experience is bad. Significant and important parts of both the game and its story are no longer available. What is here is full of ups and downs, quality is all over the place. You're not going to get the full Destiny experience that I and many others had because it doesn't exist. Destiny is a very flawed thing, but I'm so glad I got to have the experiences with it that I did.

I'm going to recommend it anyway. Load in, do whatever the game throws at you, get some gear, and when you've got a feel for it, get some friends or LFG for a raid or dungeon. Blind, with a veteran guiding you, or after watching one of Datto's guides, it's an incredible experience.

Despite it all, some of the old magic is still there for those coming in new.

I have rewrote this comment like a hundred times now. So F' it. Play this game, it's not perfect but if you look in the right spots you'll find communities and players that will make this game more than "Just a game". IMO this game is worth it. Don't be intimidated by the huge price of entry from the DLC part, it's not needed, and you can get it pretty cheap when on sale, or not at all. The build crafting is amazing, make your guardian your own, or find some content creators. Those guys always have some crazy builds that will change the way you play the game. Also check out Light.gg. This website will allow you to look up weapons and see what perks (what's good and what's not). Just know that this takes info from what players are using. Also shows you what God rolls are (weapons with certain perk combinations that is desired by the community) At the end of the day, there are some amazing gamers that play this game. I have met many of them in Destiny 2 LFG server on discord (I used this to find players wanting to do the same content I do)

I know reading the paragraph above I have mentioned finding outside info from content creators, website like light.gg (you should also look up Destiny 2 Item Manager DIM, just type that in the google search) and discord servers like Destiny 2 LFG. Look I said the game isn't perfect, but if you can get use to these tools, find a community, and overcome the challenges this game will throw at you. You will feel like you have truly accomplished something amazing. Just look at how players react to getting certain exotics and players that are able to complete a day one raid. 

I hope you challenge yourself and find a clan/friends to enjoy this game to its fullest.

See you starside, guardian.

The steam hours I have on this game does not come close to the amount of time I've spent on both D1 and D2 combined. I joined this franchise back in late Taken King where I spent hours in the Playstation Demo version of the game before my younger brother found TTK collection in Unclaimed Baggage for $20 and got it for me as a birthday gift. This franchise is one of the few good memories I still have with him before his time in juvie changed him.

I love this game. I love this franchise. I love the gunplay, the worldbuilding, the enemies, and I especially love raids and dungeons. The memories I've made on with this series has gotten me through the toughest of times in my life. When I was going through some difficult times in highschool, I would play Destiny to help myself forget the stress of adolescence and the existential fear of growing up. When I was struggling with depression, I would play Destiny to distract myself from the thoughts that plagued my mind. Hell, after each shift at my job, I would play Destiny to help me blow off steam. This game has done a lot for me and I'll never forget it.

That's not to say there hasn't been any lows in my life Destiny couldn't fix, many of which were caused by Destiny or by the constant ups and downs of adult life. Curse of Osiris, Season of the Worthy, Season of the Plunder, Season of the Deep, Season of the Witch, Episode: Revenant, and especially the disaster that was Edge of Fate. During those times, I would go to other games like Warframe, The First Descendant, etc, but they never could come close to scratching that itch like Destiny could, and Warframe especially is a really good game, so that says a lot about how much of an impact Destiny has had on my life.

And, while I've been disappointed with those listed times of Destiny's life, I can hardly blame the game for what the developers of Destiny, Bungie, have put into it and handled it. I hate Bungie. Not just because I disagree with them on issues they, frankly, should not be talking about outside their personal & private twitter accounts (looking at you, dmg), but because they took what was their golden goose and completely fumbled it. Marathon was the tip of the ♥♥♥♥ sundae but it was not the start of Destiny's downfall. No no no no, that can be attributed to the disastrous launch of base Destiny 2 and the horrendous decisions that were made during the games development. Rather than learning from their prior mistakes regarding Destiny 1's launch, they repeated it with far worse results. Fixed rolls, double primary, and limited weapons that very much was meant to appease the PvP community that were ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ about the plague of special ammo weapons like shotguns and sidearms in Destiny 1. Yet, even they were unimpressed. So you're left with a pissed of PvP community wondering why they'd bother playing crucible when all they'd earn it static gear with static rolls and weapons that were either good or not without the random rolls to help diversify their kit. Story was awful and the only good thing, the Leviathan raid, still had the same issue of no random rolls on their gear, leaving PvE players just as pissed off as the PvP players, if not more so because all those changes made to Destiny 2's sandbox was meant to appeal to the PvP community. 

There's more I could say, but this review is already getting to be too long and I have work soon so if this is goodbye, I just want to say thank you to everyone in the community that has helped me stick it out for these past 10 years of my life. Thank you to the devs that helped in creating this amazing franchise. But most importantly, to Bungie's higher ups and every employee that spent more time on twitter talking about things that neither concerned them nor did they clearly understand rather than work on the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ game: go ♥♥♥♥ yourselves, preferably with a wooden spoon.

See you all star side.
One of the best games ever created. Sad to see it end, feels like such a bad decision.

With the Final live service update just being announced, I thought it would be time to write a review. 

Even thought I started my Destiny Journey on a whim after hearing about the passing of the great Lance Redrick and the last time I played it was during the COVID pandemic for like 10 hours or so on Xbox. This game grew to become a second home i've never expected to get. In the last few years it felt like I grew with the game, met so many close friends and connections, made countless memories from insane LFG runs, Clan Raid nights, and one on ones with my friends to chill out for the night. 

Destiny offered me an environment I never thought I needed, so many people that I've met in this game have shape to who I've become more than any other social outlet, it became appart of my identity and it made me a better person overall. And although its sad that we won't be receiving any more major updates like expansions and such, I'm happy with what destiny gave to me for the last few years. Even though people will say I joined in the dark times of destiny, I don't believe so. I believe I joined up at the best point for me and I got every penny out of this game that i've sunk into it. The connections I've made in this game will grow beyond this game and that you can't put a price on. 

I would recomend anyone to pick up this game as there is no other game that comes close to the gameplay expirience that this game offers. The buildcrafting is both simple and deep that rewards experimentation. The gunplay is again something you cannot find anywhere else. And the RADS (Raids and Dungeons) are something that makes Destiny 2 stand out in this gaming landscape. 

Although, it's sad that there won't be anymore Destiny 2 content beyond the June 9th update. I'll always cherish the memories, both good and bad, that this game provided. Thank you Bungie and all of the hard working Destiny 2 Devs that have spent so much blood, sweat, and tears to make this game happen. This was a beautiful game that I am honored to have expirienced in my lifetime. I'm looking forward to what bungie makes next and that learn from the triumphs and Short commings that this game had. 

Gratias tibi ago quod mihi hanc experientiam dedisti.

I feel for any person who hasn't had the chance to experience one of the greatest video game IP's of all time. Bungie knows how to create incredible moments with friends and has done so for the past 12 years between both games. Destiny 3, I hope to see you one day.

Can't recommend a game that paid content is deleted at some point. The content I've played while leveling has been heavily altered so my playing experience can't be played ever again. Playing here and there from time to time to see what happened to the game I once liked. Also can't recommend a game that is entering in its life support stage.

This game meant so much to me, the friends/guardians I made over the years will always be with me.  So many countless hours of grinding, but still fun running raids with the boys and losing matches in Crucible.  Lance Reddick gave his heart to this game and it showed until the end.  This game may be over, but the memories made still make this game to me one of the best.  The future will be bright, whether something else comes along or not, never forget, Eyes Up Guardians.

Destiny 2 is a mess of a game. A beautiful, broken mess. Every expansion was a roller coaster of “It’s over” to “We are so back!”. Even with all the mismanagement from Bungie, horrendous FOMO, The ever expanding eververse, this game was ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ special. I started back when this was on Battle net, before Forsaken dropped and I’ve watched this game transform so many times it’s mind boggling. 

Destiny 2 was originally designed to be disposable, to have a shelf life of maybe 3 or 4 years before they moved on to Destiny 3 but as Bungie went from having Activision as a publisher to being independent they decided / couldn’t afford to keep up that pacing and so this game mutated into an abomination long past its expiration date. The Destiny Content Vault (DCV) was originally created and implemented as part of this problem, to extend the games life as they updated the engine to squeeze more life out of this game. As a result, a large chunk of the game was ripped out and nothing ever truly filled its place. The original campaign and player onboarding experience? Gutted, instead you're dumped into a remaster of the first level in the OG Destiny and kinda just… thrown to the wolves afterwards. As far as I’m aware they never really updated that, maybe a new cutscene to show you everything you used to be able to play but now your forced to watch with zero context as well as a pop up to buy the latest expansion.

Despite all the shortcomings the gameplay was ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ incredible. Nothing has come close to replicating it, the gunplay is easily the best and most responsive gunplay I’ve experienced in a PvE shooter.

♥♥♥♥, I’m just rambling now but man… The fact that we’ll never get another raid, another day 1 where I spend 12 hours stuck on one encounter, ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ sucks man. I’ve yet to experience anything comparable to a Destiny raid in any other game. Raiding is really what made me love this game, something to put that excellent gunplay to work. I’ve met so many great people thanks to this game and even if I’m no longer close to any of them, it’s been rewarding as hell to play this game.

So… where am I going with this review? ♥♥♥♥ if I know. This game is ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥. This game is peak. Nothing will scratch that itch, nothing will make me want to book a week off work to prep for a raid. I know I won’t be able to beat it in the first 48 hours but it’s so much fun I’ll do it happily regardless. I’m glad that Destiny 2 will exist beyond the final update on June 9th, that I can still experience this but… I don’t know, it sucks that it has to end like this. Not after the release of the Final Shape where we had an excellent chance at closure but instead out of nowhere during t he mid season update to a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ Star Wars crossover expansion. 

The base game is free. Try it out with some buddies, farm some loot, run a raid and enjoy this pile of ♥♥♥♥ while you can, It got 4.5K hours of my life so far, it’s worth a little of your time too.

"Guardian... if we do not meet again, know how proud I am of what you have done."

For all its tremendous ups and heartbreaking downs, there has never been a game quite as ambitious and awe-inspiring as Destiny 2, and there likely never will be again. I'll never forget the time spent in this game, the people I got to speak to, the thrills of new expansions and raids. I hope this is not the end for the Destiny universe, for the Vanguard and the Last City, the IX and the Traveller, the Guardians and their Ghosts. But the flavour text for Conditional Finality hits very hard today.

"You will dream of me... I will never leave you..."

Through all the good and bad, thick and thin I dont think there will ever be another game franchise that will draw me into its world, lore, gameplay and awe me like Destiny did. 

I still remember the first time I saw destiny alpha footage from summer of 2014 and then picking up a destiny magazine a month later then to preordering it from an EB games. I Remember 13 year old me begging to my dad to pick up my preorder after work so i could play. 

I Remember the first time launching the game and choosing warlock and getting to the devils lair stike on the first night after school and thinking wow this game is going to special. I remember my first exotic weapon and armor, Thorn from an exotic bounty which soloing at light level 26 was such a pain and finally saving up enough strange coins to buy the void fang vestments from xur. 

I have made so may friends and memories in this game its hard to think that its going to stop being updated and with no D3 in the works and with all the talk of more bungie layoffs it makes me sad to think that the IP is most likely going to be shelved. So to everyone feeling the same way "Dont be sad be happy that we all got to share this amazing game together".

I've been here since launch starting on PS4 then  moving onto PC. Those this game has had its ups and downs, it has served as joyful and amazing escape. I made many friends along the way thanks to Destiny. I will never forget the memories forged along the way.

My love letter to Destiny 2.
I love D2 so much. This game has given me so many emotions and warm memories that will stay with me forever. I want to say a huge thank you to the entire dev team, and to every Guardian I was lucky enough to play alongside.
Destiny was always there, and especially when I needed it the most. And it's hard to believe it's time to say goodbye. But this game will always have a special place in my heart.

"I'm coming home, Ace"
To the lonely, loveless, money-hungry, clueless, CEOs at Sony, I hope you never have a good night's sleep for the rest of your miserable life.

It’s heartbreaking to watch a game that meant so much slowly lose itself to decisions driven by people who probably never understood why players loved it in the first place, until eventually its life was cut short.

Destiny had its highs and lows, but I’ll never forget the community it created. A community that stayed hopeful for years, always believing things would eventually get better. I’ll never forget the excitement of Tuesdays and logging in to see where the story would go next. I’ll never forget exploring new dungeons and exotic missions, figuring out mechanics with friends, then later teaching those same mechanics to  players experiencing them for the first time.

I’ll never forget the feeling of completing my first solo flawless dungeon, then chasing another, and another after that. Or earning my first seal and watching that collection grow to 30+ over the years. I’ll never forget beating Atraks-1 on contest mode and hearing Deep Stone Lullaby for the first time. I’ll never forget organizing raid nights, or laughing until my stomach hurt because an entire fireteam got wiped by a single pendulum in Crota’s End.

And I’ll never forget Ghost. That little companion who made me roll my eyes more often than not by it's cheesy lines, but somehow always felt comforting to have around.

These are sad times but I want to remember Destiny for the memories it gave me, not the way it ended.

So before I click uninstall for the last time, I’m leaving this one final thumbs up as a small thank you for seven unforgettable years.

See you starside, Guardians.
Said it before and will say it again - Bungie should've never left Blizzard.

I absolutely loved both Destiny and Destiny 2 for most of its lifetime, having about 400 hours on Destiny 1, and 2200 hours on PC and Xbox combined for Destiny 2, I have played through everything this game had to offer, all the way up to their most recent Renegades DLC. I would say it's close to if not the game I have played the most.

Destiny 2, throughout its life, has had its highs and lows, release was not good, nor was the first two DLCs. Forsaken was an amazing return of life to the game which really brought back to how it felt to play Destiny 1. It seemed Bungie was back on the right track to make Destiny 2 better than is predecessor. Then Beyond Light released and they vaulted all of the story from the Red War to Forsaken, along with vaulting Mercury, Mars, IO, Titan, the Leviathan, and the Tangled Shore, basically tearing out half of the destinations in the game along with the story for them. This made it very difficult for new players to join in and play Destiny 2 while also keeping up with what happened previously in the world. This has not changed and by the looks of it will never change. While many of the DLCs they released since Beyond Light were amazing, along with BL itself, it does not take away from the fact they removed years of story in exchange.

Final Shape should have been the final shape of the game, they changed their DLC releases, choosing to have two smaller stories released per year (along with some mini-seasons in between those) rather than one large story in order to combat content drought. this did nothing to combat that drought, as the mini-season we did receive was barely anything, using the nostalgia of the Plaguelands of Destiny 1 to try and pull OG players back in, but only allowing access to the Plaguelands from a specific seasonal activity, with no ability to freely roam that map. The other mini-season we were supposed to get in March also got delayed to June, with the team being radio-silent about any information about its new release since then. June should have been when the next large DLC was dropping. This likely means we will not be getting that DLC until far later (if at all, considering the latest news from Sony).

It is sad to say that Destiny 2 is no longer what it once was as a powerhouse in the Looter-Shooter genre, and all that we receive now is nostalgia-bait, delays and radio-silence, along with changes to the game nobody really asked for (I'm looking at you Portal).

Destiny and Destiny 2 will always have a place in my heart as one of the first games I really enjoyed and grew with, but because of all of what has been done, I cannot recommend downloading this game nor purchasing its DLC if you want any sort of cohesive story with your looter-shooter.

Such a great and amazing game i gave over 6000 hours to but it was destroyed by Bungie. So many changes made that destroyed the game and then they threw us to the Curb for Marathon.
Eyes Up guardian! You will be missed

I used to complain about bungie selling game franchises ( halo ) now i wish nothing more then to have a game not owned by bungie, wonderful game dead due to bungie never listening to its playerbase. Rip d2

Well Unfortunately all Good Things Come to an end. i have been playing Destiny 1 and Destiny 2 for entirety of the Light and Darkness Saga. (roughly 10-11 years.) and i dont feel any ounce of Regret for Playing this ip through all that time. this game has many ups and downs as any game. and i want to say thank you to bungie and activision for bringing us a game that i have made many cherished memories over the years ive made many friends some enemies within destiny and i will never forget those people and the amazing moments we shared. and i want to put this out to the community of destiny that all of your reasons out there to dislike like love or just plain hate the game is valid. and you have every reason to feel that way between the awful treatment from the devs and lack of listening to feedback. and i believe that is what ultimately halted this game. which lead to the creativity and passion from this game being drowned out. and i hated to see it. corporate greed lay offs many controversies and well the list goes on. but we also have had a lot of good things come from this it set an example of what not to be in the video game industry. as well as fluid gameplay to rewarding activities at times to being god powered super soldiers who have toppled even the strongest of gods through out the galaxy. i miss the good times but i will always keep them with me. and its sad to see it stop and while i think we all as a community have a love hate relationship with the game its bittersweet to see it go. im happy it will be sort of time capsuled like destiny 1 because we can always come back to destiny 2 when we re feeling a little nostalgic. now that ive gotten alot of my feelings out of the way i want to put this out there for any players who are interested to play the game before or after its final update. i would personally recommend to do as much research as you can with the game between getting started and basic understanding of random rolls builds and light level. please dont put too much on yourself all at once there is alot and just as a heads up there is alot of dlc you would have to purchase in order to access a large majority of content within the game. which im sure is a big reason on why alot of people never get started along with bungies wonderful way of showing new players the game. (this is Sarcasm.) if you dont like it or think its too much then please invest your time in a different game. theres no shame in it even if your friends are telling you to play it. if someone is more than happy to guide you through it and introduce you and you would like to play that is up to you. but i will warn you once again there is alot to go through and all of the dlc on top of it is probably gonna cost 200+ if you wanna see all of it which at this time at least right now is your only way unless they decide to make all the expansions free for the last update. (doubt it.) i personally cant recommend destiny 2 for brand new players unfortunately and it hurts to say that because this game is awesome if bungie just stopped listened to what made their game so damn fun.  if anyone who is reading this new or veteran players. to the new players i hope that maybe you take that step to witness the journey we all have been through for the past decade. and to the veterans i may have not personally met you or chatted with you run dungeons with you potentially screamed at you in raids like an ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ i hope that you enjoyed your time here and it was a blast playing with you all and that all of us as guardians will not be forgotten ill see you all on June 9th . Per adaucia Ad Astra. -i will see you starside.

Broke my heart. Warframe or DBD or so many other games would NEVER abandon their player base. Especially for a crap extraction shooter that resembles nothing of the game I have played for 10 years. Everything else will pale in comparison to Destiny and will be a failure for your studio.

this game changed my life. i remember playing destiny 1, starting just before age of triumph, and i remember every single season of destiny 2 since. i made some good friends playing this game. i made many more good memories with them, and my old friends who i convinced to play it with me. i was interested in the fate saga, i loved lodi and bael and orin, and i will miss everything this game was and is and could be. no other game has or will ever come close to what destiny is to me.
this IS the best looter shooter ever created hands down, the fact theyre letting this die for garbage is insane. keep the game alive.

Through it's ups and downs, I keep coming back. There have been some monumentally bad decisions made by them along the way and those can't be defended. But what has remained true throughout is that the gameplay, the gunplay and the experiences this game has given have been second to none.

There's plenty of content to play, just there won't be any more. I'll still keep coming back, until the server's little light goes out.

With a shoddy new player experience and a reliance on others to help teach you, this is my favourite game I can't recommend to anyone, but just once I'll say it's worth it.

For almost half my life Destiny has been a part of it. I owe some of my best friends to Destiny as I met them here. Thousands of hours and countless memories, laughs and even some tears. Since the Beta days of D1 this game/games have been right along side me as I've grown up. I now have a wife, a 3 year old kid, and a house. I will never forget the sleepless all-nighters doing raids, taking time off work to play new DLC and the countless memories with friends I made that I've had the pleasure to even meet IRL. I love this game. I will forever love this world Bungie created. It means more to me than I ever thought possible for a video game. See you starside guardians. Per audacia ad astra.
