It is difficult to review a title as integral to my life as a gamer as Destiny 2. The discussions about monetization, content vaulting, and corporate interference will always be a lasting reminder of what could have been, and for many who do not read beyond the headlines, that is all Destiny will ever be. But beneath the criticism is something special: a game that created memories I will carry for the rest of my life.

At its core, Destiny 2 solved an impossible problem. Namely, how do you turn a twenty second combat loop into a decade long journey? The answer was to create the base feeling shooter I have ever played. Every weapon felt curated, every ability impactful and every expansion added new layers to a sandbox or blended gunplay, movement, and space magic in a way that has never been successfully replicated.

The sauce kept me logging in, but the experiences and memories kept me invested. I remember queuing up for the worlds first race for Deep Stone Crypt, seeing what the devs had cooked up this time as I was slaughtered without remorse. I remember the feeling of finally getting Conditional Finality after countless beatdowns of Nezarec. I remember my nerves and adrenaline of locking in at the gates of the Lighthouse for my first ever flawless as a solo and carrying my fireteam to victory on my back in that final match. But most of all i remember the victories, the wipes, and the fun of playing with my friends throughout my journey. Everyone got their hero moment, and the moments of triumph were genuinely earned.

Destiny 2 was never meant to last forever. No live-service game can. But for 10+ years it has delivered excitement, challenge, friendship, frustration, relief, disappointment and ecstasy unlike any other game i have ever played. It was perfect in its imperfection, and i am grateful for all 7777 hours alongside my fellow Guardians.

After about 2100 hrs in this wonderful, scary, sometimes very frustrating universe I guess this is it guardians. D2 was never perfect it had plenty of problems but I'll be damned if it isn't one of the most incredible things I've had the pleasure to be apart of. Love this game, thanks bungie.

years ago I started this journey. I knew nothing about this game. 
after a long time I was in my first raid. I found some good friends, 
built up a fireteam and together we fought our way in this universe.
I did my first grand master nightfall, my first solo flawless, after a time 
we fought the witch queen, conquered the dreathnought and beated all
the wittnesses disciples. we ended all the fake idols in the pantheon and 
after a long time we won the biggest fight against the wittnes in the light 
and dark saga. I´m curious about the future. I hope the 9th june wont be the end.
but even if, every end is a beginning.

what a bittersweet end to a game & series that has been around for most of my life. was never perfect, always had its rough edges, but destiny is truly one of a kind. at its best, destiny was there for all of my favorite gaming experiences of all time.

i truly, truly hope that this isn't the last we see of destiny.

"You know what's funny? I never considered how lucky I was for the time we had together. The envy of all the mothers who ever lived.

I got to love you twice."

This game has been apart of my life since it came out. I've met people in-person because of it in another country and there were 30 of us. So many memories, good and bad, but the social space it provided was amazing. If you want to go for achievements and be a complitionist this is the game for you.

Bungie has had its fumbles throughout the years, but no game strikes the same feeling as Destiny. With the game no longer releasing new content, waiting for the cost drop is not a bad idea. But if you're looking for a FPS that feels like no other game out there, give it a shot. If you can get at least 2 buddies to play even better. I've made great friends and memories with this series and will miss the new content and story dearly, but that doesn't mean I have to stop playing what I have now

I honestly never cared about the drama surrounding Destiny and Bungie. 
From the first times I played Leviathan raid back in 2019 to running raids and dungeons years later, the only thing I got from Destiny is simply lots of fun. 
Reconnecting with my brother while being heavily frustrated doing Vault of Glass, sweating my ass off doing a solo flawless Sundered Doctrine dungeon run, or simply spending evenings shooting everything in my sight mindlessly - it was all worth it. 
Yes, initially the game might be super confusing, but yet again, I don't think any other game provides the same experience that Destiny does.
Even though the support for the game ends, I know that I will still be coming back since there are still many things left to be done.
My favorite game of all time is going to die and I can't be there for it because they don't want to send an email to their anti-cheat company :(

Insane game, poured hours upon hours into and enjoyed every moment. The game had moments during it's lifecycle where things became a chore. The loot became less and less desireable but the gunplay/mystery of the world always gave you satisfaction. Even though it's been announced that it will be entering maintenance mode, you should absolutely pick this game up if you haven't already.

Show these devs what a mistake they're making by not progressing with the Destiny IP!

An unparalleled game that perfectly blended Bungie’s signature gunplay with the rewarding progression systems of an MMO. Backed by exceptional art direction and  memorable soundtracks, it consistently delivered a compelling reason to return. Its ability to create new goals and pursuits for players, time and time again, was truly remarkable.
The cooperative content (especially the raids and dungeons) offered some of the best multiplayer experiences in gaming when shared with friends or a coordinated team. While the game experienced its share of highs and lows throughout its lifespan, I always found myself returning, eager to see how it had evolved. I feel fortunate to have been part of that journey from beginning to end.

All that's left now is to hope that one day we'll see a Destiny 3.
Play the game please Raids and Dungeons are like zombie easter eggs but 5x better

Sony/Bungie may have their heads up their asses at the moment with their plan to end live service, but if you want a game with with a lot of depth, character building, build crafting, and laughs with friends, this is the game for you. 

See you starside, Guardians.

#wewantdestiny3

Nothing else compares to the level of comradery I've found during my time playing this game. 

It's not just a game it's my Destiny (2)
By far the best worst game to ever exist. Still enjoyed each minute of it. Shame the series had to go out the way it did. RIP

I used to love the story of this game before the Final Shape. Ngl, later it was only a downfall. I stoped to play before the Subway DLC. Still, it was a beautiful experience, and I'm gonna miss it.

Shame on you Bungie and Sony. Shame on you.

I've been playing destiny 2 since 2019 with some extended breaks. I made some of my best friends, made some of my most cherished memories, and have built up a colossal catalog of stories from this game. This is more than a game to me. 

Watching bungie fumble the bag time and time again has been enormously frustrating and seeing the end of service announcement was heartbreaking yet not unexpected. This is a fantastic game with endless potential run by a team of passionate devs being led by one of the most corrupt and greedy executives in the entire industry. 

If you've never played destiny before and are looking to give it a shot, now is the time. No more fomo, content complete, just enjoy the game. Progress at your pace, take in the sights, enjoy the world we destiny vets have called our home for nearly a decade.

See you starside, guardian.

So Destiny 2 is ending. It turns out the real treasure were the friends we made along the way.
I haven't played the last expansion, not because of Destiny, because my friends didn't play.

I still don't think it's a bad game, actually it is a great game. Just not for solo play.
If you and your friends are looking for a worth while time sink and Destiny is on sale grab it and play it.

I made some of my best gaming memories in Destiny. And I'm happy I got to play the Light & Darkness Saga with my friends :-)
Didn't expect it to end like this, but here we are. Cheers to our brothers and sister, Mentors and pupils, and to the good ol' days. Long may the Light shine!

For almost 10 years I've been playing this game on and off riding the highest peaks of the game and the lowest blows of them too. From spawning for the first time on the Cosmodrome as a Hunter back in Destiny 1 to now still being that Gunslinger of my group in Destiny 2 slaying the Witness (still miss Bladedancer 'till this day). No matter what comes in the future, this game will always have a special place in my heart and memories and I'll ride it until the servers collapse. 

Let me end this with a quote from the game for Sony:
"I have no reason to believe you are anything other than 'the wrong hands.' You now face godlike judgement. May it extend eternally." - Clovis Bray

The Destiny Universe and story that has been brought to us over the years is so captivating and enjoyable! The amount of love that I have for this universe is something that I'll never find anything anywhere else. The Community is so amazing and welcoming for players of all experience levels.

Destiny 2 has been a game I love to hate, so why am I recommending it? Well, I this game is truely one of a kind, unmatched gunplay, story, raids, dungeons, PVP so many activities to jump into even despite the infamous content vault. I hated on this game, not blindly, not influenced by others, but ironically, from my love for the game. Destiny 2 has always been one of the most fun games I've had the pleasure of experiencing, even through out its ups and downs. No matter the state of the game, there has never been a title more satisfying, no modern campaigns with such compelling writing and epic moments, no characters who are as interesting, likeable, even hateable. This games catalog of characters leave little left to be desired, sick af, tragic, fun, mysterious, more words that don't come to mind right away... Build crafting, game feel, competitive PVP and PVE activities/events, challenges and powerful but more importantly fun loot to chase all wrapped up in a FPS game a personal favourite style of game.

My gripes with the game are commonly shared, I believe I just noticed it earlier than most and yet I'd find myself coming back every other year. Destiny is ending its live service updates soon after this review is posted, but that does not mean the game is over. Finally so many of my issues are being resolved, unfortunately it comes as a final send off. Strangely, I find myself with regrets regarding Destiny not with being more vocal about my issues on the game, but instead I regret not playing more. I told myself time and time again, I wont play unless there is new sandbox updates, abilities added, maps and activities returned. I bet on the idea that not playing would eventually lead to the games leaders finally acknowledging player feedback and that once that happens the game would continue in a much more positive light than ever and in a sense, it happened a massive update promising updated activities, loot, abilities, returning raid encounters, new mechanics adding incentive to old locations... At the cost of being the end of service, the game will become the looter shooter it was meant to be, free from FOMO tactics, no more temporary seasons, no more unobtainable loot.

With the future of Destiny as a IP in Sonys hands, it is looking bleak. The ideas that I had believed only partially coming true continuation through Destiny 2 expansions or even a successor seems unlikely, but it is entering a state where I can see myself investing hundreds more hours into... This game is 100% worth your time if your interested, even in its fragmented state the pros heavily outweigh the cons.

To put it simply, Destiny 2 is the best bet I've ever lost...
Curious about the game? Please try it. Lets hope this final update brings a new approach towards a future installment of Destiny.
Per Audacia Ad Astra.

I've sat on the idea of writing this review for a long time. Not out of uncertainty, but out the implication that writing a review for something this important to me could be done. This is the culmination of my entire history with the Destiny franchise. Take from it what you will <3

I wasn't the most avid player of the first Destiny. A few friends and I played the beta, thought it was promising, picked up the game, played through a good-sized chunk of the story and shelved it for League of Legends (tragic). I was pulled back in by the Rise of Iron expansion and the announcement of Destiny 2. I was primarily a PC gamer at the time, but turned on my Xbox and played through the journey of becoming an Iron Lord. It was great! I loved the story, the weapons were cool, and I had fun with some friends that I didn't get to play with on PC.

Sometime after, I got into the beta for Destiny 2 on PC, and my mind was BLOWN. The weapon feel blew my mind (in classic Bungie fashion) and the story was FASCINATING. We have to regain our light? From a Cabal leader gone rogue? Count. Me. In. The base game felt solid, the exotics were fun, HOWEVER the base game left something to be desired. I completed the story, but as someone who was a casual player I never felt compelled to experience raids.

Then Curse of Osiris. My friends and I were, to be blunt, heartbroken by this bite-sized dose of mediocrity. This feeling persisted for far too long. I set the game down, resumed my regularly scheduled torture on Summoner's Rift, I would occasionally check-in with the game, curious as to how things were going... and then the DLC was announced. Forsaken.

Destiny 2 Forsaken was one of the single greatest experiences in video games, point blank, period. They brought back so many things that people loved about the first game, introduced an element of High Fantasy in the Dreaming City, and was the source of the greatest raid race to ever take place in this game: the Last Wish. This was a high unlike anything the game had ever seen, and it established Destiny 2 as a main game for my friend group for YEARS to come. 

Those years were a blur of special seasonal events, story beats that kept you coming back, and the platinum standard of what an FPS and MMO could be. Rediscovering the weapons of old in the Black Armory. Rescuing one of the most iconic characters to ever see the franchise in the Season of the Risen. Tapping into the darkness for the first time in Beyond Light. Evem further toppling the Hive Pantheon in the Witch Queen. The collective mourning of our beloved Commander Zavala's voice actor, Lance Reddick. Mastering the Strand of reality itself in Lightfall. The satisfaction of achieving closure on a 10 year story in Final Shape. The things that followed were okay, but it was easy to see the decline in quality, even from a biased lens.

Were there low points? Of course there were low points! I'm not going to stand here with 5k hours under my belt and pretend every last moment was interesting! For Christ's sake, half of the game I originally bought doesn't even exist anymore. I'm so happy that I got to experience it myself, but it's a disservice to the general player population that they cannot.

Were there high points? More than I could ever hope to count. The moments I shared with my friends over these last 10+ years are times I wouldn't trade for ANYTHING. The late night raids, the "one last run" before bed that magically teleports you to 2am, sneaking the Bungie Livestream for the DLC reveal on my breaks. This game is a part of me, and I feel as thought I'm a part of it.

This game wasn't just another looter shooter... this game set the platinum standard of music, weapon feel, and raid design that I fear would take a MIRACLE to come close to emulating. Some experiences on their own may accomplish one of these things, but all three? That's just the Bungie Magic at work, baby.

I don't know if a Destiny 3 would ever be in production... but God, at least I have this to look back on and revisit with my friends. Thank you, Bungie Devs... for an experience I'll never forget.

Per Audacia Ad Astra.

p.s: bring back Wrath of the Machine or I riot.

I woke up 12 years ago... in the Cosmodrome. I tried for a long time to understand why I was back alive and what was going on. I've met many Guardians.. close friends now. And found a new home at the Tower.
Together we've defeated countless threat to Humanity, with Hope for the Futur.

I can't express enough my gratitude for all the memories this game gave me. You had to be there to understand why this game was, and still is, perfect.

What killed Destiny, never was the players and the community. And Destiny will forever be remembered by those Fireteams and all the Guardians of Sol.

But the most important that we have to remember is, that despite everything, all the mistakes, all the flaws, we decide our own fate.

Eyes up Guardians... We, have become Legends...

Through the ups and downs its been a good run overall. Sad to see it go out like this. Destiny raids and story are some of the best content you'll ever experience in gaming period. Sucks to see an IP with such potential shelved. I blame the Bungie leadership that had the largest hand in getting to this point, and Sony for abandoning it and not wanting to take a chance on D3. I've met some of my best friends through Destiny, and experiences I'll never forget. Thank you to the devs and people who actually cared. See you starside.
